abstract: A key whose value is the signing date embedded in the code signature.
overview:
## Discussion

The value is a [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CoreFoundation/CFDate] object. Note that a signer is able to omit this date or pre-date it. Therefore, this is not necessarily the date the code was actually signed. However, you do know that this is the date the signer wanted you to see. Ad-hoc signatures never have secured signing dates.

Specify the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecCSSigningInformation] flag when calling the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecCodeCopySigningInformation(_:_:_:)] function to get this information.

# kSecCodeInfoTime

**Global Variable**

A key whose value is the signing date embedded in the code signature.

## Declaration

```swift
let kSecCodeInfoTime: CFString
```

## Discussion

The value is a [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CoreFoundation/CFDate] object. Note that a signer is able to omit this date or pre-date it. Therefore, this is not necessarily the date the code was actually signed. However, you do know that this is the date the signer wanted you to see. Ad-hoc signatures never have secured signing dates.

Specify the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/kSecCSSigningInformation] flag when calling the [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecCodeCopySigningInformation(_:_:_:)] function to get this information.
title: kSecCodeInfoTime
